title: Hawke's Bay on Budget
meta_desc: Visit Hawke's Bay with your family without breaking the bank. Discover free activities, affordable accommodation, and budget-friendly tips for a fun-filled trip to Napier and Cape Kidnappers.
intro: Hawke's Bay is a hidden gem for family travel in New Zealand, offering a unique blend of Art Deco architecture, stunning natural beauty, and exciting activities. With some planning, you can experience the best of Hawke's Bay on a budget of $180-340 per day for a family of four. From free walks to affordable accommodation, we've got you covered.

sections.heading: Understanding Daily Costs
sections.body:
A family of four can expect to pay between $180 and $340 per day in Hawke's Bay, depending on their choices of accommodation, food, and activities. Accommodation can range from $100 to $250 per night for a family-friendly motel or apartment, while meals can cost between $15 and $30 per person for a mid-range restaurant meal.

Activities such as the Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony tour can cost around $40-$60 per person, although some free activities like the Napier Art Deco walk and Marine Parade are available.

sections.heading: Free and Low-Cost Activities
sections.body:
Hawke's Bay offers a range of free and low-cost activities that are perfect for families. The Napier Art Deco walk is a must-do, with its stunning architecture and beautiful waterfront location. Marine Parade is another great spot, with its sunken gardens, free fountains, and skate park.

Waimarama Beach, located 30 minutes south of Napier, is a beautiful black sand beach that's perfect for swimming and sunbathing. The beach is free to visit and offers a range of activities, including surfing and fishing.

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body:
Hawke's Bay offers a range of accommodation options to suit all budgets. For families on a tight budget, consider staying in a motel or apartment in Napier or Hastings. These can cost between $100 and $200 per night and often include kitchen facilities and free parking.

For those with a bit more to spend, consider staying in a mid-range hotel or resort. These can cost between $200 and $350 per night and often include additional amenities such as a pool and restaurant.
list: Mid-range hotel or resort: $200-350 per night

sections.heading: Budget Breakdown
sections.body: To help you plan your trip to Hawke's Bay, we've put together a rough 3-day budget breakdown.
